business.headline: Bringing mothers in as co-applicants: Redefining home loan ownership in IndiaFamilies are now increasingly choosing to involve mothers in building long-term financial assets and securing a stronger financial future for themselves.

Sensex sinks 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Benchmark indices fell sharply on Thursday as higher crude prices, foreign selling and weak banking stocks hit sentiment. Investors stayed cautious ahead of key Q4 results, with traders watching US-Iran tensions and oil moves.

I broke my FD 3 times: When it helped and when it hurtA fixed deposit feels safe because it is supposed to sit quietly in the background, growing slowly over time. Even small savings can become the reason for family disagreements if there is no clear-written instruction from the owner.

Can senior citizens above skip ITR filing in FY27? Here's who qualifiesFiling income tax returns every year can be stressful for many elderly taxpayers. But did you know some senior citizens above 75 may not need to file ITR in FY27 at all if certain conditions are met?

Bought a car above Rs 10 lakh? You may be missing this tax creditCar buyers paying over Rs 10 lakh are being reminded to check the 1% TCS collected at purchase. The credit, linked to PAN records, can reduce tax dues or increase refunds at ITR filing.

Aashka Goradia and Brent Goble welcome second baby boy, reveal his adorable name TV actor Aashka Goradia and husband Brent Goble have welcomed their second baby boy. The actor shared a heartwarming family picture and expressed gratitude on the social media.

Dhurandhar 2 completes 50 days of box office carnage, endless global records Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge has completed 50 days in theatres with Rs 1,791.55 crore worldwide. Its sustained run and record haul have pushed it to No. 2 among Indian films globally.

general news.headline: Vijay gets Tamil Nadu Governor's nod to form government, oath at 11 am tomorrowTVK chief Vijay will take oath as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister at 11 am on Saturday after formally staking claim to form the government before Governor Rajendra Arlekar. The TVK secured the support of the Congress, Left, VCK and IUML, taking the total number to 120, just above the 118-majority mark.

Live: TVK extends its majority as 2 MLAs of DMK ally IUML back Vijay's partyTamil Nadu Government Formation News Live Updates: Tamil Nadu's volatile post-election political situation has eased after the CPI, CPM and the VCK announced its support for Vijay-led TVK. With the TVK achieving the magic figure of 118 MLAs, he is all set to take oath at 11 am on May 9. However, there was a lot of drama before the Tamil Nadu governor okayed his claim to form the government. Earlier, DMK spokesperson TKS Elangovan said the party had not discussed any alliance with the AIADMK and would wait until May 10, the deadline reportedly given by the Governor, to see whether TVK chief Vijay can secure majority support. He indicated the DMK would consult allies only if alternative political arrangements become necessary. But AIADMK dismissed reports of a DMK-AIADMK understanding as "just rumours." Meanwhile, VCK leader Sinthanai Selvan criticised TVK's approach to alliance-building, accusing some party functionaries of political arrogance and warning that opposition unity was essential to prevent what he described as BJP-backed Governor's rule. Congress MP Manickam Tagore also attacked the BJP, alleging attempts to delay the formation of a government despite what he claimed was a clear public mandate in favour of TVK. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Bhoy out, bhorsha in: Suvendu Adhikari's message after being picked as Bengal CMWith Union Home Minister Amit Shah announcing Suvendu Adhikari as West Bengal's next chief minister, the BJP is set to form its first-ever government in the state after a historic electoral sweep.

Why Indian Army officers used English, asks Pak Army spox, gets trolled by PakistanisLt Gen Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry, the head of the Pakistani military's media wing, has questioned why Indian military officers were speaking in English while briefing on Operation Sindoor on its first anniversary. Pakistanis trolled Chaudhry, questioning English usage in Pakistani military documents instead of Urdu.

India's dropout crisis: Almost everyone begins school, but few reach Class 12India has achieved near-universal access to schooling, but millions of students fail to complete their education. Data shows a sharp fall in participation beyond upper primary, exposing weak transition systems. The latest NITI Aayog report reveals how enrolment success masks a deeper crisis in retention and long-term student engagement.

Live: Suvendu Adhikari meets Bengal Governor RN Ravi, stakes claim to form govtWest Bengal New CM Live Updates: Following a several-day deliberation, Suvendu Adhikari, the BJP leader and MLA representing Nandigram and Bhabanipur, has been officially named the next Chief Minister of West Bengal. The decision was finalised after he was elected leader of the BJP's legislative party, with Union Home Minister Amit Shah formally announcing his name for the CM's post. In his statement, Adhikari expressed his gratitude towards Shah and committed the incoming administration to realising Prime Minister Narendra Modi's vision and honouring the historic mandate provided by the citizens of Bengal. The swearing-in ceremony is scheduled for 11:00 am tomorrow, and live coverage of the event remains available through Indiatoday.in.
